item_code: Dynamics of the hallucination | No object constancy
excerpt: I could see the sun rising and then setting, rising and then setting in the background of this picture.
and in my state of heightened imagination
I was completely overcome with hallucinations.

shimmering outlines of black snakes slithered around me. Out of the corner of my eye, I witnissed my nearby desk transform into a black, stone statue of a panther...Faces, hordes and hordes of faces, cascaded my field of vision like some otherworldly waterfall
the hallucinations got severely more intense than before.
but my ego was still connected to me (albeit in a fragmented fashion), and this alone served to relax me

At this point I tried to put the mental clarity of the mushroom high to good use, and essentially tried to kill my panic with rational thoughts. 'Why are you scared, why? Enjoy this, its not harmful, you're in no harm, enjoy it! ...fascinating how your mind is generating all of this! Don't be afraid!'
everything seemed crystal clear; I was still very much in control of my physical actions and had a great deal of restraint and discretion.
was finally beginning to regain contact with my ego
I found myself lying on my bed, almost completely re-attached to my ego

My mind felt as though it was failing, as though it was viewing that which it was not equipped to handle or comprehend, and I just lay there, shivering, convusling and completely gone from this world.

My sense of time was completely devastated; I clearly remember struggling to remember the properties of 'time' and the consequences involved with its passing, but all I could see were four seemingly inconsequential, random numbers glaring at me.
The next few hours are somewhat hazy in my mind, but I faintly recall a very gradual lessening of effects
I still don't know if I slept or not between my euphoria and this next period, and will never know.
and every single component seemed to be a breathing
The hallucinations were most decidely Indian in nature.
their sinister smiles
some unnamed third party was controlling the freakshow kaliedoscope that had become my thoughts.
mutating undescribable _things_ stared back at me.
and felt sheer excitement..I had scarce time to enjoy this effect
I managed to remain relatively calm

It was the sort of sheer, unadulterated bliss poets only dream experiencing. It was a happiness free of reason, free of origin, free of logic, free of limitations, free of time itself...I couldn't stop smiling..It was the strongest sense of fulfillment and perfection..
I don't think I've ever felt so comfortable and happy in total darkness as I did in those few minutes, and I'll never forget how strong and confident I felt.
I felt growing anxiety within me, and the very fact that I was anxious served to frighten me even more. I was going up ridiculously fast
All of this was simply too much for me to stomach
what finally ended up freaking me out was the idea of my parents or brother barging into my room
Alone in the darkness, convulsing wildly from the energy my body was overflowing with, I was completely overcome with hallucinations.
each of them tormenting me with their sinister smiles and cartoonish expressions.
the very fact that I had tried and failed to relax my anxiety only made everything a lot worse, as I felt more helpless than ever.

Having been driven psychotic watching my ego so brutally and quickly turned to a puddle of proverbial shit. My ego was in its death throes, and I was completely overwhelmed by the paradoxical nature of my situation.
I felt as though I had gone completely insane
The sheer oddity (for lack of a better word) of what I saw was enough to produce intense, all-consuming fear. I lay and watched, in stark horror

My mind felt as though it was failing, as though it was viewing that which it was not equipped to handle or comprehend, and I just lay there, shivering, convusling and completely gone from this world.
energetic in the extreme
I felt the most extreme, all-consuming euphoria possible.
and was completely overwhelmed by my euphoria
I fancy I may have drifted off into the forgiving hands of sleep for an undeterminable period of time, but I will never be quite sure.
If I stared at the street or any patterned surface for a few moments, it began to roll and stretch like a calm sea.
I couldn’t see or make out colors or shapes; everything was a black and brown haze
I noticed that the stall walls were bending and rolling like sheet metal.
the wood grain was moving, and upon closer inspection, the lines were stretching and shrinking
Everything was kind of glassy.
the lights began to shimmer and fade
and all the sounds of people talking ceased to be words and turned into pure sounds flowing all around and through me.
Speaking and hearing spoken words began to feel somewhat alien
I remember running my fingers across my face and feeling them pressing against my skin even after I pulled them away. They left tracers of tactile sensation.
As I was walking down a hallway, it suddenly shifted and started to rock
For just a few seconds, I felt like I was really there. Like I was in prehistoric earth in front of a T-rex

My perspective on the world began to break down. I couldn’t think of a fish as just a fish anymore. Now it was a part of an entire ecological system, and it was made of millions of molecules, and had all kinds of tissues and a distinct evolutionary history.
I began having very profound thoughts. I couldn’t think of a fish as just a fish anymore...and had all kinds of tissues and a distinct evolutionary history
The processes of my mind could not keep up with all the questions I had and everything I wanted to know.
My mind was running at 9000 rpms... I had delved into my own thoughts and was furiously plowing through them...I just kept furiously thinking about anything and everything.
I felt somewhat dreamy and floaty. I felt happy and energetic as we went off to explore the island.
I remained very happy and upbeat
I found that to be pretty amusing.
I felt very tranquil, and I noticed that the ripples in the water looked very cool.
I was super calm now
It was all so neat, and at any moment I couldn’t decide to further explore what I was working on, or try something new.
I was exhilarated at the new world around me.
I had never felt so alive or so in touch with reality around me.
The taste of my skin was amazing, and all my senses were heightened and running in overdrive.
I felt very happy.
I began to feel nervous.I was afraid my friends would find out and look down on me.I began to tremble and feel very claustrophobic.
I was kind of bummed and frustrated
I went in and felt somewhat paranoid. The place was mostly empty, and I didn’t like people looking at me.
and it about scared the shit out of me.
it felt more overwhelming than anything I had ever felt before. It was almost scary.
I felt like I had had enough, and I was agitated, and I wanted it to be over.
I did not feel tired or drained, and I followed the experience up with a night of heavy drinking.
I just felt sort of dazed. Like when I am coming down off of good marijuana, and I'm just kind of out of it.
The boundaries between everything were breaking down. All of my previously held beliefs were subject to radical questioning - even the laws of physics
and I began to see the limits and problems with language.

I realized that everyone has their own unique perspective that defines how they interpret that world, and it is extremely difficult to give that perspective to someone else to make them understand how you interpret the world. I felt like nothing was worth feeling bad about.

I realized that my thoughts up to this point had been so confined and boxed in, and I had just blasted open the doors of perception and consciousness. I hit a single point where I realized that my life would be different from this day forward, and everything was going to be wonderful
The planetarium was where I felt like my life changed...My trip was a very powerful experience, and I consider it to be one of the key events in my life that define me as a person.
Everything seemed so huge and involved, and there was so much information to know. If I saw a black chair, I immediately thought, “Why is it black? Why is it a chair?
Everything seemed so huge and involved, and there was so much information to know. If I saw a black chair, I immediately thought, “Why is it black? Why is it a chair?
I didn’t lament that I was cold; I decided to explore that feeling and see what it was all about.

item_code: Modal status of the hallucination | Impossible event
excerpt: I could actually sculpt three dimensional objects.. rather sculptures of pure light and revolving spheres, towers of emerald surrounded by throbbing orbs of sound and love.
At first I was captivated by its fluid motions and methodical actions. It was moving in rythyms, doing a dance of sorts
The visions were plentiful
I became aware that through a bit of imagining, or some similar process, I could arrange them to my own satisfaction. I found that different arrangements produced different mental states
I thought I was dead. I simply knew that I had done it, and I was somehow in a galactic prison, or a purgatory perhaps.
I wasn't sure if I had a body or not, but this thing was doing something to ME, which was still intact.

I wondered later if I really met him or not. I also wondered what entheogen or technique took us to the same place. It occurred to me a month or so later to wonder if he had seen a college honkey, stoned to the gills on mushrooms, floating through his own sacred space.
It should be added that at this point I was so immersed that it did not matter if my eyes were open or closed.
I knew I had done it to myself, but could not for the life of me remember what I had taken, why I was dead, or how it happened
Eventually an entity came and delivered me unbidden personal attention
this thing was doing something to ME, which was...Its many arms worked up and down my existence, probing and testing every bit.
it seemed to put no effort into comforting me, yet it did through some sort of telepathy imply that it would be easier for both of us if I stopped struggling. Eventually I did, and it left.
They seemed to give me a grinning, sarcastic sneer as they drifted past me.
They assured me that I was safe, and really a nice guy to boot...they sang a song revealing to me not only my own nature, but that of all creatures as well.
the elves were rapidly and almost violently competing for my attention, each trying to show me a better toy than the last.
this incredible sound caused them to order themselves into intricate yet subtle patterns of the greatest coherency.
These were the toys I presented back to the machine-elves.
I was quite relieved to see another creature
and felt pleased with the immediate physical results.
I finally relaxed, enjoying the inevitability of it all.
I started to laugh out loud, mostly at the absurdity of it all. My laughing became uncontrollable...And I laughed... I could not stop!

This was the most satisfying, absurd, and enjoyable feeling I have ever had in my life. All frustrations associated with inability to express myself were flattened. It was as if I were vomiting my soul right into the air, where it loved to dance and play.
I vomited violently...my own teeth started to vibrate horribly
I spent some time in this suffering, progressively more terrifying state.
that the 'dance' it was doing involved horrifying probes of my own form, and that it was moving faster than I could comprehend while doing so. I was paralyzed
retreated to the nearly pitch-black bedroom to lie down
I lost almost all physical awareness, and felt my mind drifting through something resembling outer space.
I wasn't sure if I had a body or not, but this thing was doing something to ME, which was still intact.

they sang a song revealing to me not only my own nature, but that of all creatures as well. They assured me that my DNA was not only similar to their own, but part of as well as *encompassing* their own 'code'. They stressed the simultaneousness of this seemingly contradictory statement.
I knew I had a brain, and a pair of lungs. I thought that was all.
some I had known while others were wildly strange.
My hand and the hand I was holding both looked and felt like dragon's claws
my bedroom wall, etc., they were familiar but distorted in space, as if my brain simply refused to do geometry right then

My sense of time was cyclical, rather than linear, throughout. That is, each moment felt connected to the one before it, but I couldn't organize a series of moments into a timeline.
fractal rainbow spiral world that had a sense of order to it as well as a sense of freedom.
followed by a sense that anything and everything was funny
I laughed a lot about my non-frogness.
That was a pleasant sensation
remember thinking that I might drown because I was so congested and so helpless in the physical realm
There was a phase when the universe felt chaotic
thing I noticed was relaxation
I noticed that I needed to pee, and decided there was no point trying to get to the bathroom. I couldn't even tell whether I was already peeing or not
at that point I fully let go of my physical body

By then I couldn't keep track of my physical body at all. I tried to blow my nose, and gave up because I couldn't relate the position of my hand to the position of the handkerchief that was within easy reach a moment before.
It was obvious to me that "all" really meant all, that there was no being or pattern of any kind that was not involved in the same healing work as me.

I had a sense that the space was infinite, that I was experiencing only one perspective on one bit of an infinite fractal universe....There was no master creator of the universe; the substrate, the hardware that the universe was running on, was consciousness..that was all about healing and love.

and I felt myself becoming part of a vast network of dancing pale green branches. There was no up or down, and no edges, my limbs merged seamlessly into the network and it was all dancing in time with the music.
At some point before my sense of my physical body completely disappeared, I seemed to be feeling only my fascia, and it seemed to be spread out in a network several metres wide.
I had a vague centre, but no edges.
The lights were dancing and Jimi Hendrix on the wall was a three dimensional mosaic doing his own undulating dance

item_code: Modal status of the hallucination | Impossible event
excerpt: when I turned my gaze, darkness followed behind
just grooving on my own beauty in the mirror.
The insects were humming...part of the symphony.
And the music was awesome.
and even the noises of cars in the distance were part of the symphony
I could feel my hair on my shoulders and it felt so sensual.
I remember this part as if it were a dream; by the time I could have described it with any clarity it was already swirling away. Really need to get a notebook or voice recorder set up next time.
The trees welcomed me.
to where the full moon was rising between the trees - oh sister moon! We shined on each other for a while.
Of course the music meshed with and shaped reality, and helped a lot to quell the anxiety.
The open eyed visuals were so cool. Everything throbbed with significance
But it was very nice. I liked the stillness.
I noticed it was stuffy and too familiar, but cozy and wonderful as well. And the music was awesome.
It was so beautiful.
suddenly cold. My feet were cold, I was shivering, but I figured it was mostly pre-flight anxiety which is par for the course.
The familiar disorientation and self-doubt rose up...'I'm not worthy' bullshit.
I got up and with difficulty
Lying in bed quietly with eyes closed

I could see that I am nothing, not so much that I am everything though. I felt like I'd been spun so gently up through the clouds into the ether of nothingness that there wasn't a moment of change from something to nothing - there is definitely a farther frontier of nothingness to experience.
The center of 'me' became thinner and thinner until I was transparent.
looking around at the room in wonder. The world was enchanted.
every outline in my entire visual field began to become wavy and almost look as if it was melting.
began to notice intricacies and patterns I had never seen before in things from the way his room and the things in it were built, all the way to different designs in the wood grain of his pool table.
had a brief thought that I looked more animalistic for some reason, almost mousy..stared at my reflection until the shadowing and even expression and outlines of my face began to change and distort
turned my head to look above my shoulder and the couch looked as if it was the size of a skyscraper. Either that or I was extremely tiny.
At this point everything in my visual field was breathing and objects became much more animated. Colors began to pop and change faintly
his thumb appeared to be inches from my face and in line with the rest of my fingers even though he was at least six feet away
by the still distorting houses and telephone poles.

item_code: Modal status of the hallucination | Impossible event
excerpt: I began to see another set of my fingers above my current set of fingers and stared at them in amazement. They weren’t connected to my actual fingers but they were very blurry and floating there just inches above.
yet as I was seeing it now it looked especially beautiful, I couldn’t take my eyes off it.

My head began to become flooded with thoughts at speeds I would have never thought possible...I would not only think of the word word and what it means but I would instantly think of every possible use...as if my brain was somehow able to access information and memories as speeds I never thought possible and I could articulate anything at lightening speeds.
I continued to talk extremely fast elaborating on a multitude of things.
It was at that very moment, that I knew it was real life
Losing touch with reality is something I would have never even thought was possible. I was in an existence where I completely thought I was dreaming, even though I was fully awake.
“Just ignore it and it will go away.” I said in full belief that my words were true…. The last thing I clearly remember was being thrown off of his couch.
They put me in the ambulance and all I could think was how this is the craziest dream and wondered when I’d awake.
I was starting to lose touch with reality more and more and more. I started to black out on and off and my memory from here on is incomplete and more a slide show of a few short scenes.

The last thing I clearly remember was.. The next flash of a scene in my memory is me looking in the mirror shirtless and bleeding from my face with my chest covered in blood. I didn’t know what was going on but I ironically thought my bleeding reflection looked like Jesus.
The next flash I am outside and the sun is rising.

It seemed to me plants have this insatiable desire to keep being, to grow, that I found truly inspirational.

the final and beautiful image I was left with was that we are all connected, every being with every little imperfection in its mightiness, and the connection I visualized was a growing mycelium in disturbed earth, branching out and connecting and sustaining even more beings at once.

As soon as I would recognize where I was, my memory would reset, and I would have to teach myself the basics all over again. To make matters worse, the ever-shortening periods between resets continually shrunk the knowledge that I could remember. In the early stages of my confusion I would look around and remember that I was at a concert with friends, that I was living on the East Coast, was engaged in a job search, and that I was originally from Colorado, before my mind would reset and I would have to start over. Near the epicenter of my confusion I would be questioning why I was surrounded by people when my mind would reset, thus preventing me from making any progress whatsoever.
For the next series of events I am forced to rely on the anecdotes of my friends who were sitting nearby.
I had come full circle, and was finally resting in the comfortable, euphoric, and highly energized state of mind that I had initially enjoyed at the very start of the concert.

I started to panic and immediately went into emergency mode, sitting down in my seat and cradling my head in my hands. I reassured myself with the voice of an experienced traveler of the depths, but I was unable to accept what I had to say. My fate was sealed, and there was nothing I could do about it.
I had come full circle, and was finally resting in the comfortable, euphoric, and highly energized state of mind that I had initially enjoyed at the very start of the concert.

They took turns explaining to me, in slow, methodical discourse, that a race of humans in another dimension had been buoyed by psychedelic experimentation and had evolved new mental capacities. They pointed out that these evolutionary advances were not spawned by genetic predestination, but had rather been induced by combinations of free-will exercises and psychedelic experimentation. They told me that I had been chosen to be one of their representatives in the earthly dimension, and that my order was to spur the evolutionary progression of my people as expeditiously as possible. It was my job to have my neighbors ready, with the necessary sets of faculties and mental tools, at the desired point in space-time...They then explained that the state of confusion I had suffered earlier in the day had been a byproduct of a recalibration scheme, in which the spirits had adjusted my core frequency to allow my senses to overlap dimensions, thus rendering me more accessible to their calling. Throughout this conversation I questioned the voices constantly, and, at no effort of my own, I was always answered in a thorough and timely fashion.

It was a happiness free of reason, free of origin, free of logic, free of limitations, free of time itself (the very same properties attributable to the anxiety I had been experiencing only a few minutes before)

As I was walking down a hallway, it suddenly shifted and started to rock, and the lights began to shimmer and fade.

item_code: Type of visual alteration | Full-blown immersive hallucination
excerpt: Eventually an entity came and delivered me unbidden personal attention. I was quite relieved to see another creature, for I suspected I was one myself (although not sure). At first I was captivated by its fluid motions and methodical actions. It was moving in rythyms, doing a dance of sorts. Eventually it occurred to me that the 'dance' it was doing involved horrifying probes of my own form, and that it was moving faster than I could comprehend while doing so.

item_code: Type of visual alteration | Full-blown immersive hallucination
excerpt: I eventually slipped into another 'room'. This was the typical round room deep psychedelics take me to. However, this time it was much larger than normal. Around the perimeter flowed the forms of creatures who looked more like cartoon drawings of dogs than anything else. They seemed Mayan, in as far as they all had dragging tongues and eyes which looked only backwards. They seemed to give me a grinning, sarcastic sneer as they drifted past me. Meanwhile, in the middle of the 'sphere', I had other entities to deal with. I cannot come up with any words to describe most of them, although the frivolous doodles which cover the margins of my school lecture notes come closer than anything at approximating their forms. The only clear example I can present is one interesting specimen: I saw a Mexican man, dressed in traditional Huichole garb, kneeling and vomiting on himself.

excerpt: I felt myself becoming part of a vast network of dancing pale green branches. There was no up or down, and no edges, my limbs merged seamlessly into the network and it was all dancing in time with the music.

item_code: Modal status of the hallucination | Impossible event
excerpt: I spent the rest of it in a beautiful fractal rainbow spiral world that had a sense of order to it as well as a sense of freedom.
I seemed to be feeling only my fascia, and it seemed to be spread out in a network several metres wide. I remember thinking that the medicine was untangling it for me. That was a pleasant sensation.
at that point I fully let go of my physical body.
The first clearly psychedelic effect was a shift in my sense of space and my own body.

I remember noticing a paradox, my perspective being the only one that was real in that moment, and that moment containing an awareness that all other moments and perspectives were also real. Other consciousness was both less real and no less real than my own. I was also aware of consciousness as the living, growing, healing, learning edge of the pattern....I had an intuition that the whole fractal was generated by some extremely simple principle that was contained in every part of it. There was no master creator of the universe; the substrate, the hardware that the universe was running on, was consciousness, and the only program it ran was a very simple one that was all about healing and love.
The lights were dancing and Jimi Hendrix on the wall was a three dimensional mosaic doing his own undulating dance,

I was sort of hoping, waiting for a dramatic moment of ego-death, and there was nothing so clear cut. Just this thinning and thinning as the cosmic winds blew through the center of where I had been until there was nothing. I could see that I am nothing, not so much that I am everything though. I felt like I'd been spun so gently up through the clouds into the ether of nothingness that there wasn't a moment of change from something to nothing - there is definitely a farther frontier of nothingness to experience. But it was very nice. I liked the stillness.

I began to see another set of my fingers above my current set of fingers and stared at them in amazement. They weren’t connected to my actual fingers but they were very blurry and floating there just inches above. As soon as I could think to tell Dee, I see from my peripheral vision, him staring at his hand in amazement on the other side of his couch and all of a sudden his thumb appeared to be inches from my face and in line with the rest of my fingers even though he was at least six feet away. I pulled my hand down and we laughed hysterically in excitement and awe.

When I would concentrate on something visually, every outline in my entire visual field began to become wavy and almost look as if it was melting. I also began to notice intricacies and patterns I had never seen before in things from the way his room and the things in it were built, all the way to different designs in the wood grain of his pool table.
I stared at my reflection until the shadowing and even expression and outlines of my face began to change and distort
As soon as I could agree with him I turned my head to look above my shoulder and the couch looked as if it was the size of a skyscraper.
Colors began to pop and change faintly and I had some strange appreciation with the design, architecture and aesthetics of everything.
I looked at the hedges in his yard and they spelled out the worlds Ron Regan
I figured I wouldn’t need it since I was currently dreaming
I started to black out on and off and my memory from here on is incomplete and more a slide show of a few short scenes.

The next flash of a scene in my memory is me looking in the mirror shirtless and bleeding from my face with my chest covered in blood. I didn’t know what was going on but I ironically thought my bleeding reflection looked like Jesus. The next flash I am outside and the sun is rising.
At this point everything in my visual field was breathing and objects became much more animated.
The vibrations and mood was now at its emotional peak. It was as if all our emotions, happy, sadness, love, anxiety, laughter, and tranquility, were pushed to new unexplored heights
we realized that everything is the way it should be, the bad and the good.

It was at this point I had one of the most intensely deep philosophical moments of my entire life. Somehow, this ancient and powerful knowledge was put into my mind that we are all one. Not all one in the sense that we all come from the same matter, but all one in the sense that we are all one consciousness, the consciousness of the universe, the exact way that what ever direction this existence is headed in wants us to be. This may be something one can think they understand, but it is as if there is a filter on everybody that is keeping the true beauty, mystery, and wonder of this existence from being able to be fully realized. At this moment I thought I had discovered the meaning of life and it hit me hard emotionally. Tears were streaming from my eyes as
At this moment I valued family more than I ever have. It was as if all the priorities I have were seen in the perfect order for me to be happy.

In place of things like arms and legs, this being operated solely through pure intent and meaning. It felt neither good nor evil. It simply was, and it was absolute perfection. The blues, greens, and purples of before simply dissolved and gave way to rich, awe-inspiring shades of gold and orange. It was like the most beautiful sunset you have ever seen, only the sun was alive and unmistakably looking back at you. I remember the edges of this being were hidden in darkness, and I could still see the same colors from before leaking out and back into its form

I wanted to be more certain that this was more than some random new neuronal connection, and that I wasn’t just talking to my pre-frontal cortex, hippocampus, or something.
I wouldn't say I remember more than 5% of what I was shown
This pulsing felt very comfortable and joyful.
Tears of ecstasy quickly became tears of sadness again

Either way, in my pain and struggling I was too distracted to focus properly on the experience, and all I was getting were very dark and confusing thought loops. I was angry at the psychedelic community for lying to me, then angry at myself for maybe just not being cut out for this stuff, and then finally angry at the mushroom for not giving me what I wanted. [...] I felt alone, completely defeated, and absolutely out of my league. I was genuinely worried I would be stuck in this pit of anger and sadness forever.
Tears of ecstasy quickly became tears of sadness again
I noticed I kept losing more and more control over my body and I began to roll around on my bed and drool. Quite honestly, it felt a bit like being a baby again.
I can remember that I was drooling uncontrollably as I began chewing on the corner of my blanket.

My arms were no longer my own, and my tongue moved without any input from me. The voice that came out was still mine, but it was not me doing the talking. The entity/alien/mushroom had begun to comfort me. It took my arms, extended my pointer finger, and began poking me in the face and chest while it tried to really, really drive home the point that “you are the universe.”

For the first time in my life, I managed to convince myself that life is nothing but a cosmic accident, order is simply an illusion necessary for survival, and existence is truly and completely devoid of any meaning whatsoever.

I finally understood how everything is alive, infinitely connected, and incomprehensibly hilarious and beautiful. And then I realized, everything emanates from everything else. Everything is one, on every scale imaginable, and every single thing is a cocreator in the project of being.
I could see my wife from where I was lying on the floor and she took on this sparkling halo around her whole body form. She shimmered like a mirage.
everything had taken on shimmering saturated jungle lushness

This world is as real as your reality.” [...] “It exists side by side with the world you live in but it is just as real. It was simultaneously beautiful beyond words, and utterly hideous
I don't know how long I remained there in the ecstatic stupor. Probably the best word to describe my emotional state was 'awe'.
I don't think I've ever experienced fear quite like this before. All I wanted was to be dead, to be released from the sorrow.
I became aware of my body once again, although it felt very much like a foreign object, dead, not the familiar flesh I have carried around with me all of my life.
By now my heart felt like it was going to explode, and my body was trembling from head to toe. I felt cold as ice, like every ounce of life was being drained out of me
Everyone on Earth suffers from the disease of history...

I've realized that life itself, existence itself is fundamentally a mystery, one that I have largely ignored up until recently. I've always had a interest in religion and spirituality, even entertained the notion that life is essentially spiritual, but never before have I felt such a complete conviction that what I perceive with my senses is only the tiniest shred of what is really going on all around me at all times.
Every object is a story in itself, every object has a history that reveals its development into the present state.

It has become all-too-clear to me that for my entire adult life I have been running away from everything that is good. I've unconsciously pushed away everything that is wholesome and uplifting. My family, my friends, my lovers, I've hurt and betrayed them all, not directly through hostility, abuse or infidelity, but through the rejection of their love, or the incapacity to really feel it and appreciate it. I thought that I was seeking knowledge or some kind of window into absolute truth, when all along I have really been seeking to rid myself of pride.
But I had never seen my room quite like this, it felt like seeing for the first time.

I put a playlist on, and it for the next hour, there was an electronic rock concert in my head - booming, immersive and wholly uplifting. I closed my eyes and flowed along with the beat and the gentle instruments that spoke of hope and excitement and things to come that were out of this world. Even the gloomier tracks failed to bring me down, instead bringing me into a more awestruck and 'deeeep' state of mind.
It wasn’t exactly closed-eye visuals… it was more than my visual imagination had sharpened by a factor of ten. Anything I thought of appeared behind my eyelids, vivid and psychedelically colourful.

It seemed I was trapped in this human form for now, but how had I ended up here, and where was I going? These questions plagued me as I paced the room. I decided the fungus I’d consumed must have liberated me from earthly existence, and that I would soon incarnate as another being, hopefully to have a more exciting and fulfilling life this time.
I had no idea what was going on. Didn’t know my own name. Didn’t know my gender or species. Didn’t know what the door was. I’d lost everything.

I oscillated between these viewpoints every ten seconds, each time convinced of it, for the next half hour or so. I knew I was stuck in a thought loop, but couldn’t do much about it. It was manic depression on speed.

It had probably been months since I ate those mushrooms; I was now in a mental asylum, in permanent psychosis, with my family and scientists looking on. Behold: the freak who fucked himself up on drugs.
And I continued to waver between remembering who I was, and believing I was God trapped in this room, this purgatory, for all time.
I finally started to remember who I was, and that I’d taken two grams of magic mushrooms.
And I continued to waver between remembering who I was, and believing I was God trapped in this room, this purgatory, for all time.
I felt inebriated. I reported this to my roommate, amazed and excited
felt assured that the next six hours would be pure awesome.
What followed was one of the most brilliant experiences of my life. I was sure I'd remember this as the most fun I'd ever had, and regard it as the best decision of my life.
I was open to the emotions in the music like never before, merged with it, and at the same time so happy, so damn alive. This, I knew, was what it meant to really live.
I dropped onto the bed, hallucinating behind the eyelids, thoroughly overjoyed to be happy for the first time since, well, the only decent DXM trip I ever had.
That in mind, I started experiencing my body in a new, and uncomfortable, way.
I felt a vague dread at this, and there was also some apprehension in the background,
I felt so ashamed and stupid.
It consisted of me lying in bed, feeling awful.
There was a buzz around my head, like it was heavier, expanding...

I was starting to get a very warped sense of my own body. [...] That in mind, I started experiencing my body in a new, and uncomfortable, way. Every sensation, and often they were dysphoric such as gastric pain, became zero-dimensional, not confined to physical boundaries – it would be nonsensical if they were. I also stopped naming them. They were just... things... that were happening. This disconnection from my body quickly drew me into a bizarre state of mind.

I realised that, indeed, our sense of things happening on different parts of our body is a sort of illusion. The feeling itself, of pain, heat, touch, etc. are pure sensations, without locality, just experiences.
I was beginning to understand that everything from my human life – my name, my friends, my every perception – had only ever existed in my mind
These sensations, and thoughts, neither of which were confined to a body, were all I really was. Therefore, I deduced, I must be one with everything. And that would make me... God.

I would press my fingers into the desk, and I perceived the resistance between my fingers and the desk as something that wasn’t happening in Euclidean space. It had no location. It was just a feeling.
This disconnection from my body quickly drew me into a bizarre state of mind. I reasoned that I was pure experience, without dimension, without boundaries.
It seemed I was trapped in this human form for now, but how had I ended up here, and where was I going?
Whenever I conjured up a friend’s face, or anything at all about my human life, it all seemed utterly ridiculous
I had no idea what was going on. Didn’t know my own name. Didn’t know my gender or species. Didn’t know what the door was. I’d lost everything.
I was experiencing severe derealisation. I was real, but the external world, and even my own body, was not
It had probably been months since I ate those mushrooms; I was now in a mental asylum, in permanent psychosis, with my family and scientists looking on.
I looked at various objects and couldn’t discern whether they were real or in my head.
But my head was still a mess. I was having trouble believing I wasn’t God, and the lack of hallucinations made me still suspect I was in a mental ward months after the actual trip.
I watched my newspapers on the table as I ate start to defy gravity and the newspapers started flapping gently, like a breeze was allowed through a close window.

I pulled back the bedsheets from my face and took off my eye mask to reveal my room again, drenched in the shimmering watery light of a beautiful sunny spring day. But I had never seen my room quite like this, it felt like seeing for the first time.
It was then that I realized that it was my turn to die.
Pardon the cliche, but I truly felt as though I had just been reborn.
I don't know how long I remained there in the ecstatic stupor. Probably the best word to describe my emotional state was 'awe'.
I don't think I've ever experienced fear quite like this before.
I became aware of my body once again, although it felt very much like a foreign object, dead, not the familiar flesh I have carried around with me all of my life.

I was paralyzed. I became aware of a sudden urgent need to relieve myself, but there was no way I could get out of bed. Embarrassingly, without warning, my bladder simply released, and I wet myself for the first time since I was a small child.

Everyone on Earth suffers from the disease of history. Every act is an act of healing. Life on Earth suffers from a great collective wound, which consciousness itself has inflicted upon all people. Life is the process of healing this wound

History is the Expanding Mind. You have seen it, you are it. This Mind has been dragging itself out of the quagmire since the beginning of time. Suffering is real. There is no such thing as a blank slate. Every being sees through a spectrum of blindness. You have been spared from suffering so that you might see the mystery more clearly. This mystery is contained inside of you, and your very existence is the only proof you need.
all that is required to know the present state of the world is curiosity, because there is meaning in literally every single object, and they are all related to one another in some way.

I've always had a interest in religion and spirituality, even entertained the notion that life is essentially spiritual, but never before have I felt such a complete conviction that what I perceive with my senses is only the tiniest shred of what is really going on all around me at all times.

It was then that I realized that it was my turn to die. It was as if I was supposed to be this afraid, because the fear itself is what would ensure that I suffered, ensure that I embraced death properly. For it is not death we fear, but the fear of death.
I always thought that death would feel like a sudden severing of reality, like entering into a void or infinite space, but no, death has many layers, death is an arduous process.
In this life, everything comes at a price. You have been shown mercy so that you may help those who are less fortunate.
You think the measure of a being its capacity to know, but you are incorrect. The measure of a being is its capacity to love
It has become all-too-clear to me that for my entire adult life I have been running away from everything that is good. I've unconsciously pushed away everything that is wholesome and uplifting.
But I had never seen my room quite like this, it felt like seeing for the first time. Pardon the cliche, but I truly felt as though I had just been reborn.

I seem to have spontaneously acquired a new kind of sense for symbolism and meaning. In art, in literature, slogans; it's as though what once appeared as two-dimensional words and pictures without significance suddenly has a three-dimensional story to tell.

The difference now, after having these two earth-shattering experiences, is that I've realized that life itself, existence itself is fundamentally a mystery, one that I have largely ignored up until recently.

I couldn't keep myself from describing out loud the absolute beauty of this night.
the sun was shining beautifully in the mountains
I looked at the various toiletries in the pantry with fascination
We all watched, fascinated, as she spun her lights about and made beautiful patterns and orbs with her photons.
the birds sang to us while we slowly enjoyed the walk home
I found I was able to tune out the cd and play a guitar beautifully
Hopping felt more exhilerating and fun than doing so on a trampoline
Running was even more exciting than hopping. We ran so fast, the world seemed to just fly by us. My legs didn't exist for a few moments as I floated up the road
As I ate more mushrooms, I felt a sort of electric jolt in my mouth whenever the taste felt strongest. It seemed to come and go in waves, truly amazing.
We were whispering to each other very conspicuously, and we were laughing hard at the fact that she probably knew we were on drugs.

The feeling was very spiritual and serene

we were laughing hard at the fact that she probably knew we were on drugs

the sun was shining beautifully in the mountains, and the birds sang to us while we slowly enjoyed the walk home.
I began to worry that I might get frostbite and that worry manifested in the form of some paranoia.
I felt like I had completely lost control of myself and began to panic.
My legs didn't exist for a few moments as I floated up the road
my fingers were growing and shrinking.

We ended up in the bathroom, all three of us looking in the mirror and studying our faces. My pupils appeared to be growing and shrinking freely, without any relation to each other. The end of my nose grew longer, then swelled like it was being inflated, then returned back to normal.

Never in my life have I felt another psychic and spiritual connection with other people like what we felt that night. Next time I want to eat an eighth and see what happens. I think if everybody could handle a mushroom trip and tried it, the world would be a better place.
We reached the tourist area and marveled at the unusually large size of the empty parking lot.
The stars appeared to me as a spacey purple color, and they twinkled like never before!
By now we were all tripping hard and laughing loudly at many things which normally aren't that funny. Everything was funny to us.
we marveled at the fact that we all saw the same distortion simultaneously.
There was one white house with a particularly bright porch light that drew us all toward it like moths.
Somehow I managed to pluck the strings accurately and my mind was able to produce music of an unusually structured nature.
we found it impossibly amazing that so many things could happen in twenty minutes.
I looked at the various toiletries in the pantry with fascination
This brought on revelation about my total change of perception, and utterly amazed me.

The sky appeared to have small white strands (like strands of cloth that come out of your clothing) and they were all moving together in the sky to form into a ball of light somewhat like the sun but as a soft white - blue. I remember the day having no moon or sun out and a clear blue sky but I was told it was a very cloudy day
I was having more visual trips of blurriness and colors changing, not mixing.
Life was the same but made with my own laws of physics and nature, faces had changed, and every room I couldn't see had to be a room in my house at home… if that makes sense to you…
Every time I turned my back from this person in my room it would switch between a little 12 year old really annoying girl and my mother.

When I slipped out of consciousness, I saw my life flash before me and all my friends and relatives waving good-bye, crying and grieving….It was at this point that I came to the grim realization that I was dead

As I moved towards the end of this corridor…. This wind force was the only thing in the universe that was able to stop me from the dragging force of death. The wind sent me flying back and as I was taken back, I returned to consciousness.
There was a force that was dragging me down this corridor, which was obviously the force of death.
I then saw this great wind force approach me as it moved around the right corner side of the T-Junction.
There was a force that was dragging me down this corridor, which was obviously the force of death.

I could sense a very horrible, terrifying feeling that was eminating from the left of this T-Junction. I became aware that this path obviously led to hell as the oppression that came over me was pure evil.

All the feelings I felt from looking to the left instantly diminished into nothing when I looked to the right side of this tunnel. The right side of the tunnel was overwhelming and suppressing the left. It was so much more powerfull.
It was approaching with...awesome power and force .  I was completely awe struck by this incredible force.
When this wind force hit me..The feeling of the love that God had for me was so powerful, it was way beyond my rational comprehension
I instantly turned from a feeling of terror to a strange sense of peace.
As it approached, I was not afraid. I was completely awe struck by this incredible force.
I was overwhelmed with complete peace, comfort and security unlike anything I had ever experienced.
I instantly turned from a feeling of terror to a strange sense of peace.
I could sense a very horrible, terrifying feeling
I was also in a state of euphoric joy.
